summary: Initial decompilation work for the ifmagnify interface module. The PR introduced src/melee/if/ifmagnify.c, matched ifMagnify_802FB6E8 in C, moved the global ifMagnify_804A1DE0 BSS object out of assembly into C, added the new C object to obj_files.mk before the remaining ifmagnify assembly object, and expanded ifmagnify.h with an initial struct layout for the magnify HUD/global state.
what_changed: Added a new C translation unit, src/melee/if/ifmagnify.c.
Implemented ifMagnify_802FB6E8 in C. The function calls ifMagnify_802FC998(slot), returns ifMagnify_804A1DE0.player[slot].state.unk when that check is nonzero, and otherwise returns 0.
Declared and defined the global ifMagnify ifMagnify_804A1DE0 in C, replacing the assembly-side .bss allocation of 0xF0 bytes.

Expanded src/melee/if/ifmagnify.h from simple prototypes into an initial ifMagnify struct definition using HSD_Joint, HSD_GObj, HSD_TObj, HSD_ImageDesc, a per-player array of six entries, and bitfields for player state.
Updated function prototypes in ifmagnify.h from u8 parameters to s32 parameters for ifMagnify_802FB6E8 and ifMagnify_802FC998.
Removed the assembly implementation of ifMagnify_802FB6E8 from asm/melee/if/ifmagnify.s.
Promoted several local labels in ifmagnify.s to global function labels: ifMagnify_802FB8C0, ifMagnify_802FBBDC, and ifMagnify_802FC3BC.
Updated assembly references that previously targeted local labels .L_802FC3BC, .L_802FB8C0, and .L_802FBBDC to use the new global symbols.
Added $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/if/ifmagnify.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk immediately before the remaining asm/melee/if/ifmagnify.s.o.
smart_moves: Started with a small, isolated matching win: only ifMagnify_802FB6E8 was moved to C while most of ifmagnify.s remained intact.
Defined the ifMagnify_804A1DE0 global in C with a struct sized to the original 0xF0-byte BSS allocation, preserving the data layout while enabling typed access.

Used comments in the struct to document which remaining assembly functions imply particular field types or offsets, such as ifMagnify_802FC3C0 accessing the joint and tobj fields and ifMagnify_802FBBDC accessing an ImageDesc.
Split former local assembly labels into global symbols so remaining assembly code can safely reference routines that are becoming independently named/decompiled functions.

Inserted the C object before the assembly object in obj_files.mk, allowing the C definition of the earlier symbol and BSS global to replace removed assembly while leaving the rest of the module linked from assembly.
Modeled the byte at the per-player state offset as bitfields, allowing the matched C expression state.unk to compile to the same low-six-bit extraction behavior seen in assembly.
decomp_lessons: When matching a function that reads a packed state byte, struct bitfields can express clrlwi/rlwimi-style flag packing in C, but the bit ordering and field width must be validated against emitted code.
For gradual asm-to-C migration, it is acceptable to move a BSS symbol into C once the C struct is sized and aligned to match the original assembly .skip allocation.
Remaining assembly may need local labels promoted to global symbols when C object files or other assembly references require stable symbol names.
Header work can be productive even before full decompilation: an initial struct layout based on observed offsets gives later functions typed field names and reduces raw offset reasoning.

Use known baselib object types in provisional structs when assembly call sites reveal the data kind: HSD_Joint, HSD_GObj, HSD_TObj, and HSD_ImageDesc were inferred from access patterns and function usage noted in comments.
Keep unknown padding explicit, as with u8 unk[0xF0 - 0x74], to preserve total global size while only naming fields supported by evidence.
naming_conventions: The module keeps doldecomp address-based names such as ifMagnify_802FB6E8, ifMagnify_802FC998, ifMagnify_802FB8C0, ifMagnify_802FBBDC, ifMagnify_802FC3BC, and ifMagnify_804A1DE0.
The new struct type is named ifMagnify, matching the module/global naming style.
Per-player state fields use descriptive provisional names where evidence exists: is_offscreen, ignore_offscreen, and unk.
Unknown fields use conventional placeholder names such as x4, x8, xC, x10, unk, and state.unk.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched ifMagnify_802FB6E8 by reproducing the assembly control flow: call ifMagnify_802FC998(slot), branch to return 0 on false, otherwise load the per-player packed state byte and extract its low six bits.
The struct layout places the player array so player[slot].state.unk corresponds to the original indexed load from ifMagnify_804A1DE0 plus slot * 0x10 plus offset 0x20.
The per-player entry size is 0x10 bytes, matching the assembly's slwi slot, 4 address calculation.
Bitfield layout is used to make state.unk compile as the six-bit value after two one-bit flags.
The assembly .bss definition for ifMagnify_804A1DE0 was removed after adding the C global definition.
Remaining assembly callback/function-pointer references were changed from local labels to global ifMagnify_* labels so symbol references remain valid after partial migration.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue decompiling src/melee/if/ifmagnify.c.
Check whether the ifMagnify struct field names or bitfields were later renamed or corrected.
Search for later changes to ifMagnify_804A1DE0 size, alignment, or player entry layout.
Search for eventual C matches of ifMagnify_802FB8C0, ifMagnify_802FBBDC, ifMagnify_802FC3BC, ifMagnify_802FC3C0, and ifMagnify_802FC618.
Verify whether ifMagnify_802FC998 parameter type remains s32 in later headers.
